clusters.title: New details about Tiger Woods car crash
clusters.neutral_headline: Tiger Woods Had Prescription Painkillers After Crash

items.title: New details about Tiger Woods car crash
items.standfirst: Golf superstar Tiger Woods had hydrocodone pills in his pocket and bloodshot eyes at crash scene, say US authorities.
items.content: Golf superstar Tiger Woods had hydrocodone pills in his pocket and bloodshot eyes at crash scene, say US authorities.

items.title: Tiger Woods had prescription painkillers and failed sobriety tests after crash: police
items.standfirst: The golfer's eyes were bloodshot and glassy, his pupils dilated and he had hydrocodone pills in his pocket when interviewed at the scene of his car crash.
items.content: Tiger Woods' eyes were bloodshot and glassy, his pupils dilated and he had hydrocodone pills in his pocket when interviewed at the scene of his car crash last week in Florida, according to a sheriff's office report released on Tuesday (Wednesday morning AEDT).Woods' movements were slow and lethargic, he was sweating as he talked to deputies, and he told them he had taken prescription medication earlier in the morning, according to the incident report released from the Martin County Sheriff's Office.Deputies found two white pills, which were identified as the opioid hydrocodone used to treat pain, in his pocket, the report said.READ MORE: The government reviving a 77-year-old law to stop price gouging at the pump When asked by a deputy if he took any prescription medications, Woods said, "I take a few."Woods told deputies he had been looking at his phone and fiddling with the radio before he clipped the truck in front of him, the report said.The golfer was traveling at "high speeds" on a beachside, residential road in Jupiter Island when his Land Rover clipped the truck and rolled onto its side, according to the sheriff's office, noting that Woods showed "signs of impairment".The truck had $US5000 ($7300) in damage, according to the sheriff's report.Neither Woods nor the other driver were injured. Woods agreed to a breathalyser test that showed no signs of alcohol, but he refused a urine test, authorities said. He was arrested and released on bail eight hours later.Woods' agent at Excel Sports, Mark Steinberg, has not responded to multiple messages seeking comment.No one from Woods' camp or the PGA Tour – he is on the board and is chairman of the committee reshaping the competition model – has commented since his arrest.Woods, who has been involved in other crashes over the years, is charged with driving under the influence, property damage and refusal to submit to a lawful test.Under a change to Florida law last year, refusing a law enforcement officer's request to take a breath, blood or urine test became a misdemeanour, even for a first offence.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

ai_summary: Tiger Woods had hydrocodone pills after car crash
Woods had bloodshot eyes and failed sobriety tests
Police found prescription painkillers in his pocket
Woods was arrested and charged with driving under influence
Incident occurred in Florida last week
